title: Pungent Japanese Cheese Tamagoyaki
description: A sour and cheesy Japanese dish featuring white bread and tamagoyaki, perfect for midnight cravings. This flexitarian-friendly recipe is enclosed in a crispy en-croute.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Butter a 6-inch tamagoyaki pan or a small skillet.
In a bowl, whisk together eggs, milk, salt, and black pepper.
Add shredded cheese and sour cream to the egg mixture and stir until combined.
Pour the egg mixture into the prepared pan and cook over medium heat until the edges start to set.
Use a spatula to gently lift and fold the edges of the tamagoyaki towards the center.
Once the tamagoyaki is almost set, place a slice of white bread on top and sprinkle with additional shredded cheese.
Transfer the pan to the preheated oven and bake for 5 minutes, or until the bread is golden brown and the cheese is melted.
Remove from the oven and let cool for a minute before slicing and serving.
Garnish with chopped scallions if desired.
